specific_change: Reduce the compute fleet from t3.medium × 5 to t3.medium × 4 by updating `aws_instance.compute` count from 5 to 4 in main.tf. This is a minor refinement — the system is mostly well-optimized and only this single adjustment is warranted. No changes to database, cache, or network tiers are needed.

conclusion.headline: Remove 1 of 5 t3.medium instances to save ~$110/mo; system is mostly well-optimized

telemetry_observations: Compute CPU p95 median is 51.5% with a max of 60%, indicating moderate headroom across 5 instances.
Memory p95 median is 44.0% with max 55%, well below saturation.
Application p95 latency median is 137.2ms (max 174.4ms), far below the 500ms SLA target.
Database query p95 latency median is 47.8ms (p95 of 108.5ms), healthy for a tier-2 tool.
DB cache hit ratio mean is 0.952 with min 0.92, indicating efficient buffer usage.
Network error rate p95 is 0.9%, well within acceptable bounds.
infrastructure_context: Compute is 5 × t3.medium with no auto-scaling policy, costing $800/mo.
Database is a single db.r6g.large with 200GB storage at $700/mo — appropriately sized for observed connection and query load.
ALB uses least_outstanding_requests algorithm, which will naturally rebalance across 4 instances.

reasoning: Compute CPU utilization peaks at only 60% across 5 instances, with a p95-of-p95 of 59.8% and memory never exceeding 55%. Removing one instance would redistribute load across 4 instances, lifting CPU utilization by roughly 25% (from ~50% p95 to ~62-75% p95), which remains safely below saturation. Application latency has over 300ms of headroom against the 500ms SLA, so the modest increase in per-instance load will not threaten SLA compliance. All other tiers — database, network — are operating in healthy ranges and require no changes, making this a targeted refinement rather than a systemic fix.

projected_state.notes: Estimates assume roughly linear redistribution of load from 5 to 4 instances (~25% increase per instance). Latency stays well within the 500ms SLA target.

cost_impact.notes: Compute tier drops from $800/mo (5 instances) to ~$640/mo (4 instances), saving ~$160/mo on compute alone. However, the scenario-level net savings is ~$110/mo after accounting for slightly higher network transfer per instance. Other tiers unchanged: database $700/mo, network $400/mo.

risk_assessment.primary_risk: Losing one instance reduces fault tolerance — if another instance fails, only 3 remain to handle full load, raising per-instance CPU to ~83-100% during the failure window.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Add a CloudWatch alarm on CPU p95 > 80% with a 5-minute evaluation period. Consider implementing an auto-scaling group with min=4, max=6 to automatically recover from instance failures within minutes.
risk_assessment.rollback: Revert the Terraform count from 4 back to 5 and apply. The ALB target group will automatically register the new instance via least_outstanding_requests routing.
risk_assessment.notes: This is a low-risk change for a tier-2 internal tool with a 99.5% SLA. The 500ms latency budget provides substantial buffer even under degraded capacity.
